violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Staff informed inspector that they are enrolled in class. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2024

A bucket of soap and water was observed in the hand-washing sink, with cloths draped over splash guards. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Items were removed during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2024

The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Staff stated they used the cloths on the splash guards to dry hands. Paper towels were available on a counter across from the hand sink. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2024

Staff were unaware of how to set up the sanitizer of the 3-bay sink. Inspector demonstrated during inspection, including how to test for proper concentration of chorine. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2024

Pre-cooked hard-boiled eggs were observed not marked with the date they were taken from packaging. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2024
Establishment does not have drain plugs. Drain plugs were obtained from the other location during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2024

The chlorine concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). The concentration of chlorine in the sanitizing solution must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC diluted to proper concentration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2024
